Title: Functions of Arabidopsis CTP:phosphocholine cytidylyltransferase 1 in phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis and root growth

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CTP:phosphocholine cytidylyltransferase; CCT family; contains catalytic domain C, αE region, J segment, membrane-binding domain M with auto-inhibitory region
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane phospholipid important for membrane functionality and stress responses; supports root growth under osmotic stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in developing siliques and seedlings/roots under normal and osmotic stress conditions
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Phaseolin (seed-specific, RNAi); tCUP3 (constitutive, RNAi); Glycinin (seed-specific, overexpression); CaMV 35S (constitutive, overexpression)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in cct2 knockout background; transgenic overexpression complementation; lipidomics by LC-MS; root phenotype analysis under 200 mM mannitol; recombinant protein enzyme assay; circular dichroism; structural modeling and molecular dynamics; phosphomimetic mutant analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; osmotic stress induced by 200 mM mannitol; lipid vesicle treatment in vitro (PC:PA)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphatidylcholine accumulation and supports root growth under osmotic stress; S187 phosphomimetic mutation weakens biological function
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cct1 knockdown cct2 knockout lines showed significantly reduced total phospholipid and PC intensities in developing siliques. 2. AthCCT1 overexpression restored PC intensity in the mutant background. 3. AthCCT1 deficiency caused shorter primary roots under mannitol-induced osmotic stress, and native AthCCT1 overexpression rescued this phenotype. 4. Phosphomimetic S187D/S187E variants had reduced enzyme activity, and S187D failed to rescue the stress-induced short-root phenotype.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes AthCCT1 as the main experimentally decisive gene; cct2 alone had little effect on PC intensity, and AthCCT1 S187 phosphorylation may modulate function through structural/conformational effects. Discussion notes possible moonlighting functions beyond PC homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics (UHPLC-QqTOF LC-MS); no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: AthCCT1 suppression impaired root growth under osmotic stress; no obvious developmental abnormality under normal conditions reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve phosphatidylcholine homeostasis and root resilience under osmotic stress; phosphorylation state at S187 should be considered because phosphomimetic state reduces functional complementation

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: De novo phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis via CDP-choline branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/key regulatory step in PC de novo biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key regulatory step in primary PC de novo biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers CMP from CTP to phosphocholine to form CDP-choline, an activated intermediate for phosphatidylcholine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane phospholipid involved in membrane function and abiotic stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assessed in developing siliques and seedlings/roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic knockdown/overexpression complementation in Arabidopsis; lipidomic profiling; recombinant enzyme activity and kinetic assays; phosphomimetic mutagenesis; physiological root assays under osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; 200 mM mannitol osmotic stress; lipid vesicles in enzyme/CD assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PC biosynthesis; loss reduces PC intensity and stress-responsive root growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cct1 knockdown in cct2 knockout background reduced PC intensity in developing siliques. 2. AthCCT1 overexpression restored total phospholipid and PC intensity. 3. Recombinant phosphomimetic mutants S187D and S187E showed strongly reduced catalytic activity and altered kinetic parameters. 4. S187D overexpression failed to rescue osmotic-stress short-root phenotype, linking catalytic regulation to physiological function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: S187 lies in the J segment; phosphomimetic mutation reduced lipid-induced helical change, decreased membrane-contact behavior of the auto-inhibitory region, and increased constraint between AI region and αE helix in simulations.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics (UHPLC-QqTOF LC-MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target for improving PC content and osmotic stress resilience, but native active form is preferable over phosphomimetic/inactive states
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Partial suppression caused stress-sensitive short roots under mannitol; no substantial effects on TAG or galactolipids reported

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane phospholipid involved in membrane function and stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-translational regulation of phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis via AthCCT1 activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates AthCCT1 enzymatic activity through Ser187 phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely reduces PC biosynthetic capacity and compromises AthCCT1-dependent root growth function under osmotic stress when AthCCT1 is in phosphomimetic state
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-relevant negative post-translational regulator of the key PC biosynthetic enzyme AthCCT1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior phosphorylation evidence cited; phosphomimetic AthCCT1-S187D/S187E enzyme assays; structural analyses; in planta S187D complementation test
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic stress induced by 200 mM mannitol; lipid vesicle-dependent activation context in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for expression of AthCCT1 phosphomimetic mutant in planta
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Previous work showed AthCCT1 is mainly phosphorylated by SnRK1 at Ser187. 2. Phosphomimetic substitution at this site decreased AthCCT1 activity and lipid-induced conformational change. 3. The S187D version could not rescue the osmotic-stress short-root phenotype, supporting negative regulation of AthCCT1 function by this phosphorylation state.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SnRK1 itself was not the primary direct experimental focus in the Results; included here because phosphorylation by SnRK1 is explicitly linked to regulation of core gene AthCCT1 in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid or reduce negative phosphorylation state of AthCCT1 at Ser187 when engineering PC accumulation or osmotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Negative regulation of AthCCT1 may lower stress resilience
